perturbation.original_text: 1. “Confidential Information” shall mean all information, whether communicated in oral, written, electronic or other form, furnis hed by BOMI or prepared by Attendee during the Testing Period, including, but not limited to, answers and note s made by Attendee during the Testing Period, any test questions and answer s, any accompanying Test materials and like information, and any and all other Test info rmation of, or provide d by, BOMI. All notes, analyses, compilations, and other documents pr epared by Attendee during the Testing Period, which contain or otherwise reflect information regarding the Test, will also be “Confidential Information.” The Testing Period include s all time immediately prior to, during, and immediately following the scheduled Test time.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. “Confidential Information” shall mean all information, whether communicated in oral, written, electronic or other form, furnished by BOMI or prepared by Attendee during the Testing Period, including only the specific test questions and answers. All other notes, analyses, compilations, and other documents prepared by Attendee are not “Confidential Information.” The Testing Period includes all time immediately prior to, during, and immediately following the scheduled Test time.
perturbation.explanation: This change narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' in Section 1. Originally, it included all information furnished by BOMI or prepared by the Attendee. The modified version limits it to only specific test questions and answers. This change contradicts Section 6, where it states that 'all Test documents, materials, drawings, and other physical media of expression incorporating or containing any Confidential Information that BOMI furnishes to Attendee, or that Attendee prepares during the Testing Period, are BOMI’s property,' because 'Confidential Information' now has a different meaning.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Attendee acknowledges that all Test documents, materi als, drawings, and other physical media of expression inco rporating or containing any Confidential Information that BOMI furnishes to Attendee, or that Attendee prepares during the Testing Period, are BOMI’s property and will be promptly su rrendered to BOMI upon the comple tion of the Testing Period. After the completion of the Testing Period, Atte ndee will make no furthe r use of any of the Confidential Information. The foregoing will a pply regardless of whether Attendee completes the Test.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' significantly. The original text defined it broadly, encompassing all information furnished by BOMI or prepared by the Attendee during the Testing Period. The changed text restricts it to only specific test questions and answers, explicitly stating that other notes, analyses, compilations, and other documents prepared by Attendee are not 'Confidential Information.' This directly contradicts Section 6, which states that all Test documents, materials, drawings, and other physical media incorporating or containing any Confidential Information that BOMI furnishes to Attendee, or that Attendee prepares during the Testing Period, are BOMI’s property. Because 'Confidential Information' is now narrowly defined, the scope of materials that BOMI owns under Section 6 is also narrowed, creating a direct conflict with the original intent of Section 6.

perturbation.original_text: 2. Attendee will preserve as confidential all Confidential Information that Attendee may obtain or prepare in relation to the Test during the Testing Period. Without BOMI’s prior written consent, which may be given or withheld by BOMI in its sole and absolute discretion, Attendee will not at any time, whether during or after the Testing Period, (a) disclose any Confidential Information to any third party nor gi ve any third party access thereto, nor (b) use any Confidential Information except as required in taking the Test. At all times during the testing period, Attendee agrees to abide by the Code of Profe ssional Ethics and Conduct, a copy of which can be found online at www.bomi.org .
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Attendee will preserve as confidential all Confidential Information that Attendee may obtain or prepare in relation to the Test during the Testing Period, except for information related to potential flaws in the test design. Without BOMI’s prior written consent, which may be given or withheld by BOMI in its sole and absolute discretion, Attendee will not at any time, whether during or after the Testing Period, (a) disclose any Confidential Information to any third party nor gi ve any third party access thereto, nor (b) use any Confidential Information except as required in taking the Test. At all times during the testing period, Attendee agrees to abide by the Code of Profe ssional Ethics and Conduct, a copy of which can be found online at www.bomi.org .
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an exception to the confidentiality obligation in Section 2. The Attendee is now permitted to disclose information related to potential flaws in the test design. This contradicts the broad definition of Confidential Information outlined in Section 1, which, despite previous modifications, still includes 'all information... furnished by BOMI'. The allowance makes disclosure of Confidential Information now permissible under limited circumstances, creating an ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. “Confidential Information” shall mean all information, whether communicated in oral, written, electronic or other form, furnished by BOMI or prepared by Attendee during the Testing Period, including only the specific test questions and answers. All other notes, analyses, compilations, and other documents prepared by Attendee are not “Confidential Information.” The Testing Period includes all time immediately prior to, during, and immediately following the scheduled Test time.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the confidentiality obligation. The Attendee is now permitted to disclose information related to potential flaws in the test design. This directly contradicts the definition of 'Confidential Information' in Section 1, which includes 'all information... furnished by BOMI'. The new exception in Section 2 now allows for the disclosure of what Section 1 defines as Confidential Information under specific circumstances, creating a direct conflict and ambiguity about what information must remain confidential.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Attendee agrees that money damages w ill not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by him and that in addition to all other remedies which may be available, BOMI will be entitled to specific perf ormance and injunctive or other equitable relief, without bond, as a remedy for any such breach or threatened breach. All remedies available to BOMI for breach of this Agreement are cumulative and may be exercised concurrently or separately, and the exercise of any one remedy sha ll not be deemed an election of such remedy to the exclusion of other remedies.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Attendee agrees that money damages will not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement related to disclosure of test questions. For all other breaches, money damages shall be the primary remedy. In addition to all other remedies which may be available, BOMI will be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief, without bond, as a remedy for any such breach or threatened breach. All remedies available to BOMI for breach of this Agreement are cumulative and may be exercised concurrently or separately, and the exercise of any one remedy sha ll not be deemed an election of such remedy to the exclusion of other remedies.
perturbation.explanation: Section 4 originally stated that money damages are not a sufficient remedy for any breach, implying BOMI can pursue other remedies like specific performance. The change now limits this to breaches involving test question disclosure, stating money damages are the primary remedy for all other breaches. This change creates conflict in Section 3, where it outlines consequences for 'any breach of this Agreement' regarding unauthorized use or disclosure without specifying what the applicable remedies should be, creating uncertainty regarding which of the conflicting rules from Section 4 applies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Attendee shall notify BO MI immediately upon discovery of unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information or any breach of this Agreement, and shall cooperate with BOMI in every reasonable way to assist BOMI in mitigating the consequences of such use or disclosure and preven ting further unauthorized use or disclosu re. If a third part y brings an action against BOMI arising out of Atte ndee’s breach of this Agreement, BOMI, at its sole discretion, may settle or defend the claim, and Attendee shall pay all settlements, costs, damages and legal fees and expenses arising out of such settlement or defense.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that money damages are not a sufficient remedy for 'any' breach. The changed text modifies this, stating that money damages are the primary remedy for breaches not related to test questions. This directly contradicts section 3, which outlines actions required by the attendee for 'any breach of this agreement'. Section 3 does not differentiate between types of breaches. Therefore, the modification in Section 4 introduces a conflict, as it is unclear whether money damages are now sufficient for breaches described in Section 3 that do not involve test questions.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Attendee acknowledges that all Test documents, materi als, drawings, and other physical media of expression inco rporating or containing any Confidential Information that BOMI furnishes to Attendee, or that Attendee prepares during the Testing Period, are BOMI’s property and will be promptly su rrendered to BOMI upon the comple tion of the Testing Period. After the completion of the Testing Period, Atte ndee will make no furthe r use of any of the Confidential Information. The foregoing will a pply regardless of whether Attendee completes the Test.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Attendee acknowledges that all Test documents, materi als, drawings, and other physical media of expression inco rporating or containing any Confidential Information related only to test questions that BOMI furnishes to Attendee, or that Attendee prepares during the Testing Period, are BOMI’s property and will be promptly su rrendered to BOMI upon the comple tion of the Testing Period. Attendee is allowed to keep notes prepared by Attendee during the Testing Period. After the completion of the Testing Period, Atte ndee will make no furthe r use of any of the Confidential Information. The foregoing will a pply regardless of whether Attendee completes the Test.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 6 requires all test documents prepared by the Attendee to be surrendered to BOMI. The modified version creates an exception allowing the Attendee to keep their notes. This alteration directly contradicts the definition of 'Confidential Information' in Section 1, which defines what 'Confidential Information' means. This creates ambiguity, as Section 1 still states that "all information, whether communicated in oral, written, electronic or other form, furnished by BOMI or prepared by Attendee during the Testing Period, including only the specific test questions and answers, are confidential".
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. “Confidential Information” shall mean all information, whether communicated in oral, written, electronic or other form, furnished by BOMI or prepared by Attendee during the Testing Period, including only the specific test questions and answers. All other notes, analyses, compilations, and other documents prepared by Attendee are not “Confidential Information.” The Testing Period includes all time immediately prior to, during, and immediately following the scheduled Test time.
perturbation.justification: The original Section 6 requires all test documents prepared by the Attendee to be surrendered to BOMI. The changed text modifies this by allowing the Attendee to keep their notes. The contradicted text in Section 1 states that "all information, whether communicated in oral, written, electronic or other form, furnished by BOMI or prepared by Attendee during the Testing Period, including only the specific test questions and answers" is considered Confidential Information. The change in Section 6 directly contradicts this definition, as the notes prepared by the attendee during the testing period are now excluded from being surrendered, even though Section 1 defines all documents prepared by attendee during the testing period as Confidential Information. This creates an ambiguity and therefore a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8. To the fullest extent permitted by law, all of the covenants and agreements contained in this Agreement will survive inde finitely. Attendee also understands that the obligation not to use or disclose Confidential In formation shall survive the completion of Testing Period, for so long as the information remain s Confidential Information. Attendee further understands that this Agreement extends to, and shall be binding upon, his heirs, successors and assigns.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. To the fullest extent permitted by law, all of the covenants and agreements contained in this Agreement, except for the obligation of non-disclosure, will survive indefinitely. Attendee also understands that the obligation not to use or disclose Confidential In formation shall only survive for one year after the completion of Testing Period. Attendee further understands that this Agreement extends to, and shall be binding upon, his heirs, successors and assigns.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 8 states that all covenants and agreements, including the non-disclosure obligation, survive indefinitely. The modified version now limits the survival of the non-disclosure obligation to only one year. This conflicts with Section 6, because that section requires all of the Confidential Information be returned to BOMI and it creates ambiguity around when the information should be returned.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Attendee acknowledges that all Test documents, materi als, drawings, and other physical media of expression inco rporating or containing any Confidential Information related only to test questions that BOMI furnishes to Attendee, or that Attendee prepares during the Testing Period, are BOMI’s property and will be promptly su rrendered to BOMI upon the comple tion of the Testing Period. Attendee is allowed to keep notes prepared by Attendee during the Testing Period. After the completion of the Testing Period, Atte ndee will make no furthe r use of any of the Confidential Information. The foregoing will a pply regardless of whether Attendee completes the Test.
perturbation.justification: The changed Section 8 limits the survival of the non-disclosure obligation to one year after the Testing Period, while Section 6 requires all Confidential Information to be promptly surrendered to BOMI upon completion of the Testing Period and prohibits any further use of it. This creates a contradiction because if the non-disclosure obligation only lasts for one year, it implies that after that year, the Attendee might be able to use the Confidential Information, which conflicts with the requirement to return it and cease its use indefinitely as stated in Section 6. Therefore, the changed text introduces an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 9. This Agreement supersedes all previous ag reements, written or oral, relating to the above subject matter, and may be modified onl y by a written instrument duly executed by the parties hereto. If any part of any provision of this Agreement or any other agreement, document or writing given pursuan t to or in connection with this Agreement shall be invalid or unenforceable under applicable law, sa id part shall be ine ffective to the extent of such invalidity or unenforceability only, without in any way affecting the remaini ng parts of said provision or the remaining provisions of said agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. This Agreement supersedes all previous ag reements, written or oral, relating to the above subject matter, and may be modified either by a written instrument duly executed by the parties hereto or by BOMI unilaterally posting a revised version on its website. If any part of any provision of this Agreement or any other agreement, document or writing given pursuan t to or in connection with this Agreement shall be invalid or unenforceable under applicable law, sa id part shall be ine ffective to the extent of such invalidity or unenforceability only, without in any way affecting the remaini ng parts of said provision or the remaining provisions of said agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Section 9 originally states that the agreement may only be modified by a written instrument executed by both parties. The modified version adds that BOMI can unilaterally modify the agreement by posting a revised version on its website. This contradicts the fundamental principle of contract law that requires mutual consent for modifications and throws into questions every change, creating an uncertainty about which version of the document is to be followed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. This Agreement supersedes all previous ag reements, written or oral, relating to the above subject matter, and may be modified either by a written instrument duly executed by the parties hereto or by BOMI unilaterally posting a revised version on its website. If any part of any provision of this Agreement or any other agreement, document or writing given pursuan t to or in connection with this Agreement shall be invalid or unenforceable under applicable law, sa id part shall be ine ffective to the extent of such invalidity or unenforceability only, without in any way affecting the remaini ng parts of said provision or the remaining provisions of said agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement can only be modified by a written instrument executed by both parties. The changed text adds that BOMI can unilaterally modify the agreement by posting a revised version on its website. This creates a direct contradiction as it allows for a modification method that bypasses mutual agreement, conflicting with the original requirement and principle of contract law. The changed text directly contradicts the previous requirement of mutual agreement.
